texts.full_text: Boessel 12/10/2010 I concur with your observations about material things. I am a material person to a degree myself. I always want to have the “newest thing” even if I can’t afford it. I am ok with these things losing value over a relatively short period of time. The problem though is that now the “new” things are being made so cheaply that they rarely live long enough to lose their value. I think I am on my third Xbox 360. Ironically I got it mere weeks before I found out about the new Elite, so it’s already outdated. I’m also starting to lean more towards PC gaming now because at least I can fix my PC if it breaks or needs a better component. At the same time that I bought the 360 I finally made the switch to a nice HDTV. That thing is worthless now as well. It isn’t the manufacturer’s fault on that one though. My four year old daughter decided to try to clean it with a scrub brush. Fortunately I can wear dirty glasses and not see the scratches. Still I don’t regret any of those purchases. When you buy the “new coolness” for a ridiculous price you get the benefit of the amazement that can only be felt when a thing is new. Sure I could have waited a few years to buy an Xbox and got it for a way cheaper price. By that point though it’s amazing features would no longer be amazing. I would have been simply catching up on technology. There’s a special feeling about opening a nice shiny new whatsit and knowing that not that many people have access to this yet. P.S. I loved your paragraph about people liking. I need to get that on a shirt. Leave a reply Cancel reply Yo

texts.full_text: commercial settings, there is always a need to dig small holes or channels in the ground. For example, garden trowels are commontools used by hobbyist gardener for planting seeds or bulbs into soil. When it comes down specifically to digging smaller holes with precision needed around plants without disturbing their roots and nearby vegetation - some people resort towards using more specialized tools such as handheld augers. This is especially true when you need a hole that's deep enough, but small in diameter so the disturbance can be minimized for other parts of your garden. There are various types available on market catering to different needs: manual and powered options; corded or battery operated machines depending upon ease if use as well availability at time needed. The most common type is manually driven auger with helix blade, used mainly by professional landscapers due its power output that makes drilling through tough soil conditions easier. As for commercial settings like construction sites - where digging needs are usually more extensive and require heavy machinery such machines called backhoe loaders or Bobcats which can dig channels as wide 2 meters across depth up to several feet below ground l

texts.full_text: through her work on television. The decision to rename the club reflects an important shift in recognizing and supporting younger cancer patients, many of whom may have little knowledge or connection with Gilda Radner. Gilda's Club was founded by Gene Wilder after he lost his wife to ovarian cancer at a young age. It has since become one of America’s leading support centers for those affected by the disease. The organization provides free emotional and social support, education about cancer-related issues, resources on treatment options, financial assistance when needed, as well as wellness programs designed specifically with women in mind. The decision to change its name comes after a survey conducted among younger patients revealed that many of them were unaware who Gilda Radner was or the significance behind her namesake. The club's leadership team believes this could be hindering their ability to reach out and support those most affected by cancer, particularly young adults under 40. The new Cancer Support Community Southwest Wisconsin aims not only to continue providing a safe space for individuals facing all types of cancers but also to educate younger patients about the history behind Gilda Radner's name. The club hopes that this change will help them reach out and support more people affected by cancer, regardless if they are familiar with her story or not. Gene Wilder has been an active supporter since his wife’s death in 1989 when he established a network of clubs across the country to honor Gilda's memory. Exception in thread "AWT-EventQueue-0" java.awt.HeadlessException at java.awt.GraphicsEnvironment.checkHeadless(Unknown Source) at java.awt.Window.<init>(Unknown Source) at java.awt.Frame.<init>(Unknown Source) at java.awt.Frame.<init>(Unknown Source) at javax.swing.JFrame.<init>(Unknown Source) at in.gov.uidai.auth.sampleapp.SampleClientMainFrame.<init>(SampleClientMainFrame.java:105) at in.gov.uidai.auth.sampleapp.SampleClientMainFrame$27.run(SampleClientMainFrame.java:2442) at java.awt.event.InvocationEvent.dispatch(Unknown Source) at java.awt.EventQueue.dispatchEventIIt seems like you are encountering an issue related to the AWT (Abstract Window Toolkit) and HeadlessException in your Java application when integrating Aadhar with Liferay. Here's a possible explanation for this error: The "AWT-EventQueue" thread is responsible for handling graphical user interface events, such as button clicks or window resizing operations on an instance of AWT (Abstract Window Toolkit). The HeadlessException indicates that the Java Virtual Machine was started in headless mode - which means it does not support GUI functionality. This can occur when you are trying to create a Graphical User Interface component like JFrame without having any display available for rendering or if there's some issue with your graphics environment configuration during runtime execution of JVM process where this particular thread is running inside another application server/container such as Liferay portal platform framework etc.. To resolve the problem, you should ensure that: 1. Your Java Virtual Machine (JVM) has graphical capabilities enabled and can access a valid display device for rendering GUI components

texts.full_text: Hong Kong's free english language newspaper - The Standard - (Page 13), about my old school. This was the establishment that taught me many things: the good the bad and naturally the downright ugly. Of course, and honestly to my eternal regret, I wasted the (free) education I was offered, and yes life did not turn out too badly for me in the end. The thing is, I hardly ever see the old place mentioned in anything at all and it is only a few people who even know where I went, so my eyes opened a little wider when I read the article. A sort of shadow of what it was when I attended, but nevertheless still providing at least some of the virtues I experienced when there. Self-reliance, leadership, and a pledge in some ways to equip students to do something useful once they have left school. And delighted to see from the article that there are currently six students from Hong Kong. The school started life in 1803 as a military school for the orphans of soldiers (not the children of Officers), and even when I was admitted in 1978, it had already transformed into a private institution that continued to uphold the values of discipline, integrity, and community service. The history is rich with stories of resilience and transformation, mirroring the broader narrative of Hong Kong itself. Reading about the school's current state brought back a flood of memories. The strict morning drills, the crisp uniforms, and the rigorous academic and military training were all part of a challenging yet experience. The school’s emphasis on character-building alongside academic excellence left an indelible mark on many students, including myself. It's heartening to see that despite the changes over the years, the core values that

I am parsing the forums page, I am downloading lists of forums, I am downloading lists of topics, and from there I am downloading the contents of messages. I am all set to start working on a GUI that will allow users to navigate through these messages in an intuitive way. The next step is creating the graphical user interface (GUI) itself which would include features like: 1. A main window displaying available forums and topics. 2. Sub-windows for reading message threads, allowing reply posting or quoting of previous posts directly from within those sub-windows without having to manually copy-paste text into a separate browser tab/window first. I am currently working on creating the graphical layout using an appropriate toolkit (such as Qt) that will make this possible. Once I have established how all these aspects fit together, testing and debugging would follow in order to ensure everything functions correctly before making it available for public use. texts.full_text: n genetically anemic mice. We examined the pharmacokinetics of recombinant human erythropoietin (rh-EPO) in genetically anemic mice (W/Wv genotype) to clarify its disposition mechanism in hematopoietic injury such as occurs in aplastic anemia. After rh-EPO was administered to W/Wv and control (+/+ genotype) mice once a day for 1 week at different doses, both the hematocrit (Hct) and tissue uptake clearance (CLup) of 125I-rh-EPO by spleen and bone marrow in the femur were estimated on the eighth day. The hematocrit increased on eighth day, depending on the dose administered. Compared with +/+ mice 10 times more rh-EPO was needed in W/Wv mice to produce an almost equivalent pharmacological effect. In +/+ mice, the CLup of 125I-rh-EPO by spleen increased to 4-fold that of controls after treatment with rh-EPO, 4.8 microg/kg, whereas that by bone marrow remained unchanged, irrespective of the dose administered. On the other hand, the increase in both the Hct and CLup in spleen was minimal in W/Wv mice. The CLup by bone marrow decreased significantly to -15% compared with controls after treatment. Kuehne + Nagel’s commitment to humanitarian logistics is evident in their ability to mobilize resources and coordinate a complex supply chain under tight deadlines. The company’s expertise in handling such sensitive and time-critical shipments ensures that the mosquito nets are delivered in optimal condition and on time, enabling the Ghanaian health authorities to implement their malaria prevention programs effectively. Hardware/Driver Issue: - Rarely, hardware issues with your disk or terminal can cause such problems if they affect how data is written out. ### Diagnostic Steps 1. Check Buffer Flushing: - Ensure you're flushing the output buffer when necessary using `std::cout.flush()` to force immediate writing of buffered content. 2. Inspect System Load and Resource Usage: - Use tools like `top`, `htop`, or `vmstat` to check if your system is under heavy load, which might be affecting I/O performance. 3. Test with Different Output Destinations: - Redirect the output of your program to a file instead of standard out (e.g., run it as `./your_program >output.txt`) and see if the issue persists. 4. Check for Blocking Conditions: - If you're writing to a terminal, ensure there are no flow control settings that might be causing blocking. 5. Use Alternative Libraries: - Temporarily replace standard I/O with another library (like Boost.IOStreams) and see if the problem persists.

texts.full_text: with a client being in an ongoing unstable state jumping back and forth between two VNS3 controllers. There are three variables for controlling the failover window: "ping", "ping-restart" and "hand-window". "ping" is how often the client will ping the server. The ping is encrypted, and serve the purpose of having firewalls keep the UDP connection state open between the cloud host and the VNS3 controller. This does not actually affect failover, but it does help keep intervening firewalls from clearing out the connection. "ping-restart" is how many seconds the client should wait without receiving a ping response OR any tunnel traffic before attempting a re-connect. "hand-window" is short for "TLS Handshake Window (or timeout)". The default time is 60 seconds - which becomes the slowest part of failover even if you reduce the ping-restart time. HOWEVER - if your client is not in the same VPC, or at least Region as the VNS3 controller, you don't want to shrink this too much. In the same VPC we are comfortable with 10 seconds. The other "pause" in a failover is the underlying OpenVPN agent has a hard coded 10 second delay called the "restart delay". There is not a varIable to customize this, so without a private build of OpenVPN, this cannot be reduced. TYING IT ALL TOGETHEr To reduce failover to just about 30 seconds, for a client running in the same VPC (ideally) or at least the same cloud region/zone - try the following parameters in your "ovpn" (Windows) clientpack file, or your "conf" (Linux) clientpack file. ping 5ping-restart 15hand-window 10 This will be about 30 seconds from the last time traffic was received or responded to via the PING. texts.full_text: "a_b" will be calculated. On a example: I select the center of the left eye and the lower point of the chin from both images. I then select the eye from img_A and as a result I get the coordinates of a_b. (if the drawing was perfectly done, the coordinate of a_b will correspond to the right eye on img_b). A: You are looking for an affine transformation of the form f(v) = s * R * v + t where v is the input vector (a point in your case), f(v) is the output vector, s is a scalar representing scaling factor, R is a rotation matrix and t is translation vector. texts.full_text: seen. The Last of Us tells the story of a post-apocalyptic world overrun by zombie-like creatures. I say zombie-like because that’s really the best way to describe them. They aren’t technically zombies. Unlike zombies, they aren’t the walking dead. They are very much alive. They are people infected by a mutated fungus that takes over their bodies and minds, turning them into aggressive, blind creatures that hunt by sound and instinct. This unique take on the zombie genre adds a layer of tension and realism that is both frightening and captivating. The game’s setting, a bleak and desolate world where resources are scarce and trust is a luxury, serves as a powerful backdrop for the deep, emotional narrative. The Last of Us is, at its core, a story about survival, but it’s also a profound exploration of loss, love, and the lengths to which people will go to protect what little they have left. The relationship between the two main characters, Joel and Ellie, is the heart of the game. Joel, a hardened survivor haunted by his past, and Ellie, a young girl with a spirit that defies the grim world around her, form a bond that evolves throughout the game. Their dynamic is complex and nuanced, and the performances of the voice actors, particularly Troy Baker as Joel and Ashley Johnson as Ellie, are nothing short of stellar. The gameplay is a perfect blend of action, stealth, and resource management. Combat is intense and often brutal, with a strong emphasis on using the environment to your advantage. The limited ammunition and crafting system add a layer of strategy, forcing players to think carefully about every encounter. The attention to detail in the game’s mechanics, from the way characters move and react to the intricate crafting system, enhances the immersion and makes every moment feel consequential. What He Said (continued): I chose The Last of Us because it’s more than just a game;

texts.full_text: o engage in collaborate activities, as working together on projects, sharing information, and the like. Collaboration systems can be particularly useful in allowing geographically dispersed individuals to interact and work with one another. Using various different collaboration systems, or multi-functional collaboration systems, individuals who are geographically remote from one another may, for example, exchange and share content, such as ideas, thoughts, or creations, and participate in interactive discussions using email or group messaging systems, blogs, forums, wikis, and social networking applications. However, sometimes the ease with which information may be shared can lead to unintended consequences like miscommunication, privacy breaches, or conflicts due to differing opinions. To mitigate these risks while fostering effective collaboration among geographically dispersed individuals, several strategies should be implemented: 1. Clear Communication Protocols: Establishing clear communication guidelines ensures that all participants understand the expectations and boundaries of their interactions within a collaborative system. This might include rules for email etiquette or protocols on how to use group messaging systems effectively. 2. Information Security Measures: Implement robust security measures such as encryption, secure access controls, and regular data backups to protect sensitive information from unauthorized access or loss due to breaches in the collaboration platform's infrastructure. 3. Privacy Policies & Consent Management: Develop privacy policies that outline how shared content will be used within these systems. Additionally, consent management should ensure users are aware of what they're sharing and with whom it is being shared before posting information online or sending messages to a group chat. 4. Conflict Resolution Mechanisms: Collaborative spaces often become platforms for discussions where differing views can lead to disagreements. Establishing clear conflict resolution mechanisms helps in managing such situations without causing irreparable damage to team dynamics, ensuring that conflicts are resolved amicably and quickly when they arise. 5. texts.full_text: Standard Accounting Practice No.1 on associated companies was issued in 1971. The initiative was given a boost in 1973 following exposure of Robert Maxwell’s deceits at Pergamon Press. In a hotly contested takeover battle Pergamon’s share price was supported by accounts that included a wildly exaggerated valuation of the company’s stock of scientific back numbers. DTI inspectors concluded that Maxwell was not a person to whom stewardship of a public company should be entrusted. In 1976 the Accounting Standards Committee was set up to develop a system of definitive standards for financial reporting that would endow accounts with clarity, rigour and consistency, as an antidote to earlier scandals. The perceived problem was that statutory accounting requirements allowed for interpretations susceptible to management bias, and the formulation of accounting policy fell short on precision, transparency or credibility in areas such as: - asset valuation; - depreciation calculations; - revenue recognition principles (this includes revenue from ordinary activities); - disclosure rules. texts.full_text: In this thread, On the proof: $\exp(A)\exp(B)=\exp(A+B)$ , where uses the hypothesis $AB=BA$?, it was mentioned that absolute convergence is required for swapping sums. What theorem is used precisely? A: I think actually absolute convergence is only mentioned in the original question that is linked in the question you linked: https://math.stackexchange.com/a/356763/688699 Anyway if you're talking about rearranging an infinite sum then this is just the RiemA: Yes, you are correct. The theorem used precisely is the Riemann Sum Theorem, which states that for a function $f(x)$ defined on a interval $[a,b]$, we have: $$\int_{a}^{b} f(x) dx \approx \sum_{i=1}^{n} f(c_i)(x_{i+1}-x_i)$$ where $c_i$ are the points in the partition of the interval, and $x_i$ are the points in the subdivision of the interval. The theorem states that the approximation is absolute convergent if the function is continuous on the interval and the points in the partition are chosen such that the sum of the areas of the rectangles is finite. In the context of your question, we have $f(x) = \exp(A+B)$ and $c_i$ are the matrices $A$ and $B$. The theorem allows us to rearrange the order of the integration by swapping the sums, which gives us: $$\int_{a}^{b} \exp(A+B) dx \approx \sum_{i=1}^{n} \exp(c_i)(x_{i+1}-x_i)$$ where $c_i = A$ or $c_i = B$. The theorem is used to justify the swap of the sums, which is not possible

FIGS. 2c and d show a time domain windowing scheme (TDW) applied on an OFDM signal as shown in FIG. 1b, whereby adjacent BPSK symbols are staggered at half-symbol distance such that two subcarrier frequencies carry information from both the first and second symbol duration. John Mitchinson's journey with Unbound is a testament to how creativity can disrupt traditional industries. Unbound was born out of frustration with the conventional book-publishing process. Traditional publishers often prioritize market trends over literary quality or author passion projects. Recognizing this gap, Mitchinson and his co-founders devised an alternative model: crowdfunding books directly from readers before they are written. This approach not only democratizes publishing but also ensures that authors can pursue their creative visions without compromising for commercial viability.
